subjects.name: English Language (Prelims & Mains)
subjects.analysis: RC dominates. In Mains, focus shifts to inference-based questions and complex sentence connectors.

subjects.name: Numerical Ability / Quantitative Aptitude
subjects.analysis: Arithmetic heavy. Prelims requires speed in calculation; Mains requires deep data interpretation skills.

subjects.name: Reasoning Ability & Computer Aptitude
subjects.analysis: Puzzle heavy. Accuracy in logical reasoning is the differentiator in the Mains merit list.

subjects.name: General & Financial Awareness (Mains Only)
subjects.analysis: Current affairs and banking awareness are heavy score boosters. Focus on last 6 months.

mentorship_data.title: Prelims Strategy: The Speed Game
mentorship_data.content: Focus on high-speed topics like Simplification and Inequalities first. Accuracy must be above 95% to clear high state-wise cutoffs.

mentorship_data.title: Mains Strategy: Accuracy & GA
mentorship_data.content: GA preparation is essential for the merit list. In Mains, solve puzzles selectively; avoid time-consuming complex arrangements until arithmetic is secured.

mentorship_data.title: Sectional Timing Management
mentorship_data.content: Each section has a fixed time. Practice switching mindsets between calculation and logic rapidly during mock sessions.

mentorship_data.title: Final Selection Protocol
mentorship_data.content: Only Phase II (Mains) marks are counted for the final allotment. Since there is no interview, every single mark in Mains is crucial.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Is there an interview in IBPS Clerk?
frequently_asked_questions.a: No, there is no interview for clerical posts in IBPS. Selection is based purely on Mains marks.

frequently_asked_questions.q: What is the negative marking policy?
frequently_asked_questions.a: A penalty of 0.25 (1/4th) marks is applicable for every wrong answer in both Prelims and Mains.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Are Prelims marks added to the final merit?
frequently_asked_questions.a: No, Prelims is a screening test. The final merit is calculated using Phase II (Mains) marks only.
Yes, GA carries 50 marks in Mains and is often the deciding factor because it takes less time to attempt.

frequently_asked_questions.q: Is there a descriptive test like PO?
frequently_asked_questions.a: No, IBPS Clerk does not have a descriptive (Essay/Letter) test.
Yes, candidates must be proficient in the official language of the state they applied for; otherwise, they may be disqualified.
